message:
Refactor config command from flags to subcommands

This commit transforms the `br config` command from a flag-based interface
to a subcommand-based interface, improving discoverability and aligning with
modern CLI design patterns.

BEFORE (flag-based):
  br config --list           # List all config options
  br config --get KEY        # Get a specific value
  br config --set KEY=VALUE  # Set a value
  br config --delete KEY     # Delete a value
  br config --edit           # Open in editor
  br config --path           # Show config paths

AFTER (subcommand-based):
  br config list             # List all config options
  br config get KEY          # Get a specific value
  br config set KEY=VALUE    # Set a value
  br config delete KEY       # Delete a value (alias: unset)
  br config edit             # Open in editor
  br config path             # Show config paths

Changes made:

1. src/cli/mod.rs:
   - Replaced `Config(ConfigArgs)` with `Config { command: ConfigCommands }`
   - Added `ConfigCommands` enum with variants: List, Get, Set, Delete, Edit, Path
   - Each variant carries its own arguments as struct fields
   - List subcommand now has --project and --user flags directly
   - Delete subcommand has visible_alias "unset" for convenience
   - Removed ConfigArgs struct (replaced by ConfigCommands enum)

2. src/cli/commands/config.rs:
   - Changed execute() to accept `&ConfigCommands` instead of `&ConfigArgs`
   - Replaced conditional flag checking with pattern matching on enum
   - Updated show_config() signature to take bool params instead of ConfigArgs
   - Simplified control flow: each subcommand maps directly to one function
   - Cleaner separation of concerns per subcommand

3. src/main.rs:
   - Updated Commands::Config pattern to destructure `{ command }`
   - Pass &command to execute() instead of &args

Benefits:
- Better --help output with clear subcommand structure
- Easier to extend with new config operations
- Consistent with other CLI tools (git, cargo, kubectl)
- Each subcommand can have its own help text and validation

Note: Two functions (list_options, get_config_options) are now unused
and generate warnings. These may be candidates for removal or
reconnection to the new interface in a follow-up commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -159,7 +159,10 @@ pub enum Commands {
     Undefer(UndeferArgs),
 
     /// Configuration management
-    Config(ConfigArgs),
+    Config {
+        #[command(subcommand)]
+        command: ConfigCommands,
+    },
 
     /// Sync database with JSONL file (export or import)
     ///
@@ -1158,41 +1161,43 @@ pub struct SyncArgs {
     pub robot: bool,
 }
 
-/// Arguments for the config command.
-#[derive(Args, Debug, Clone, Default)]
-#[allow(clippy::struct_excessive_bools)]
-pub struct ConfigArgs {
-    /// List all available config options with descriptions
-    #[arg(long, short = 'l')]
-    pub list: bool,
+#[derive(Subcommand, Debug, Clone)]
+pub enum ConfigCommands {
+    /// List all available config options
+    List {
+        /// Show only project config
+        #[arg(long)]
+        project: bool,
+
+        /// Show only user config
+        #[arg(long)]
+        user: bool,
+    },
 
-    /// Get a specific config value by key
-    #[arg(long, short = 'g', value_name = "KEY")]
-    pub get: Option<String>,
+    /// Get a specific config value
+    Get {
+        /// Config key
+        key: String,
+    },
 
-    /// Set a config value in user config (format: key=value)
-    #[arg(long, short = 's', value_name = "KEY=VALUE")]
-    pub set: Option<String>,
+    /// Set a config value
+    Set {
+        /// Config key=value pair
+        kv: String,
+    },
 
-    /// Delete a config value from the database (DB keys only, not YAML)
-    #[arg(long, short = 'd', visible_alias = "unset", value_name = "KEY")]
-    pub delete: Option<String>,
+    /// Delete a config value
+    #[command(visible_alias = "unset")]
+    Delete {
+        /// Config key
+        key: String,
+    },
 
     /// Open user config file in $EDITOR
-    #[arg(long, short = 'e')]
-    pub edit: bool,
+    Edit,
 
     /// Show config file paths
-    #[arg(long, short = 'p')]
-    pub path: bool,
-
-    /// Show only project config (from .beads/config.yaml)
-    #[arg(long)]
-    pub project: bool,
-
-    /// Show only user config (from ~/.config/bd/config.yaml)
-    #[arg(long)]
-    pub user: bool,
+    Path,
 }
 
 /// Arguments for the stats command.
